summary: Release-aware commands must preserve tranche semantics, source availability must follow the source registry flag, and all published docs/metadata/help text must use the ufo-goat binary/module names from the provenance manifest.
reason: ufo-goat exposes PURSUE release tranches as user-facing release numbers, while release_batch=0 is reserved for files without a derivable tranche; filtering after the FTS limit, filtering timeline rows after fetching the full timeline, or writing during dry-run can produce misleading command results. The source registry has an explicit Available flag for provisional sources, so source resolution must reject unavailable sources even if a future URL is pre-populated. The published package also ships cmd/ufo-goat-pp-* binaries under library/other/ufo-goat, so CLI help, README/SKILL install paths, manifest, GoReleaser, Makefile, and MCP docs must not point at stale ufo-pp-* names or the stale library/other/ufo module path.
